heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 553 Days: New Canciller, Transportistas Megablockade, AIFA Photo Controversy
heroSubtitle: President Sheinbaum reshuffled her cabinet on April 1, naming Roberto Velasco Álvarez as the new Foreign Minister after Juan Ramón de la Fuente resigned citing health reasons. On April 6, transporters and farmers launched an indefinite national highway megablockade in 20 states demanding action against extortion and fuel tax relief. Sheinbaum defended AIFA's Semana Santa passenger record of 164,525 travelers amid AI-manipulation allegations. The peso strengthened to ~17.83 MXN/USD and Banxico raised its 2026 GDP forecast to 1.6%. The USMCA review deadline of July 1, 2026 and the Section 301 tariff probe (Mexico response due April 15) continue to dominate the bilateral agenda.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español
